stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama-3 indicates relevance to the current state-of-the-art in large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags, including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model
stories.author: olivier_lacombe
bullet_points: Introduction of Gemma 4 12B, a new unified, encoder-free multimodal model.
The model is specifically designed for high-performance deployment directly on local devices (laptops).
This represents a significant step towards accessible, powerful on-device AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing LLM execution on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by requiring no environment variables or configuration files.
The tool supports running various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: This is your laptop… on AI
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: The current tech industry narrative is heavily focused on AI's transformative impact across all computing.
Major tech leaders, exemplified by Nvidia's Jensen Huang, are strongly advocating for AI integration into personal devices.
The trend suggests a fundamental shift in how laptops and personal computing will function due to AI advancements.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
It addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/per-repository fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to code evolution.
The key advantage is injecting repository knowledge without incurring any inference-time token overhead.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a self-evolving, multi-agent framework utilizing LLMs for automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery (MLE).
Addresses limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Positions the work as a critical step toward enabling LLM agents for complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and advanced MLE.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for Code Language Models.
This method addresses the limitations of current techniques (long input RAG or full fine-tuning) by injecting repository knowledge without inference-time token overhead.
It specifically tackles the challenge of providing code models with necessary repository-level context to handle imports, APIs, and evolving codebases.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a new LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation and memoryless search.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization necessary for complex tasks like scientific discovery.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline being showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning.
It is optimized for complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its compatibility to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: S&P 500 blocks fast SpaceX entry, won’t waive rule for unprofitable AI firms
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking SpaceX from gaining easy access to passive investor capital.
The index provider is unwilling to waive existing rules for companies deemed unprofitable, even if they are in the AI sector.
This highlights the financial hurdles and governance challenges facing high-growth, high-risk technology firms like SpaceX.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging the gap between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces 'HANDOFF,' a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to be intuitive and general for diverse manipulation skills.
Utilizes a technique involving Distilled Complementary Teachers to achieve effective control in a real-world deployment context.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for robots to switch between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages during manipulation.
Unlike prior methods that only change between fixed speeds, TempoVLA specifically explores and enables controlled deceleration in VLA policies.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the *process* of co-editing, not just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to track how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI text transformation.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential computation and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT circumvents the need for traditional recurrent credit propagation, aiming to improve long-range association learning and parallelism.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve the training process.
The work targets limitations in existing methods like GRPO, which typically rely on Monte Carlo approaches for reward assignment after a full Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trace.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded, low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The technique enhances RAG by enabling the retrieval of stronger evidence earlier in the text generation process.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, enabling fine-grained adaptation.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and intuitive command interface to replace dense kinematic references.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world document workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to improve the learning of long-range associations by making RNN training more parallelizable.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve RL training for Chain-of-Thought (CoT) models.
The work targets limitations in existing methods like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) which rely on Monte Carlo-style delayed feedback.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method for Self-Augmenting Retrieval applied to Diffusion Language Models.
Discrete diffusion models generate text by iteratively denoising tokens in parallel, committing confident predictions.
Low-confidence tokens discarded during denoising are shown to be valuable lookahead signals for improving retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method adds no inference overhead as preconditioned weights can be merged back into the original architecture.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection by focusing on the progressive human-AI co-editing process, not just final outputs.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Highly relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants in professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by iteratively denoising the entire response in parallel, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to serve as a valuable lookahead signal for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The research addresses the efficiency bottleneck in long-context inference for LLMs, particularly during complex reasoning tasks.
It tackles the common trade-off between speed (achieved by structured sparse attention) and accuracy (maintained by token sparse methods).
The proposed method, 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing,' aims to improve decoding efficiency without significant quality degradation.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'prompt skills' (like instructing LLMs to act as Popperian falsificationists) in improving code generation.
It critically examines the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge for evaluating these code generation gains, noting inherent biases in such judges.
The research questions whether observed performance improvements are genuinely due to the prompting skill or artifacts of the evaluation method.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) generation in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT forces reasoning through a slow, discrete, and serial token stream.
Latent reasoning aims to provide a higher-bandwidth method for intermediate computation, potentially handling semantic or uncertain updates more efficiently.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitation of current domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of using a single encoder for various inputs in modern audio applications relying on LLMs.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate user stances in online discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a method to audit how sensitive LLM simulations are to context changes.
The study aims to determine if LLM simulations reflect genuine user beliefs or are merely artifacts of conversational context.

stories.title: New method turns ocean water into drinking water, without waste
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: A new method for desalination has been developed.
The process claims to convert ocean water into potable drinking water.
A key feature of the method is that it operates without generating waste.

stories.title: Inference Theft: Your AI Endpoint Is Someone Else's Free Model
stories.author: morganwilliscloud
bullet_points: Addresses the emerging security threat of 'inference theft' targeting AI endpoints.
Proposes practical defense mechanisms including bot detection and guardrails.
Recommends operational strategies like cost-aware routing and budget controls to mitigate attacks.

stories.title: AI needs a brake pedal before the next model jump
stories.author: jenueldev
bullet_points: The article emphasizes the critical need for safety controls in advanced AI models.
These controls must allow users to slow, scope, or completely stop powerful AI systems.
The discussion frames AI safety as a practical engineering requirement rather than purely theoretical.

stories.title: Is MCP Dead? When the Model Context Protocol Earns Its Complexity
stories.author: contrite42
bullet_points: The article addresses the debate surrounding the viability of the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
It highlights that token cost is a significant issue, but Anthropic's code-execution fix drastically reduces this cost (by 98.7%).
The piece analyzes the specific scenarios where MCP's complexity remains valuable despite cost concerns.
